* [EngSys] Agentic Workflow tweaks

The focus of these changes is to tune behavior of
the issue triage and samples/docs workflows, to
remove friction and enhance functionality.

Issue Triage:
  - If labeling was successful and a package was
    identified, validate on NuGet that the package
    is not deprecated. If it is, inform the issue
    author and resolve the issue.

Samples and Docs:
  - Revise the workflow to open an issue with a
    detailed set of instructions for a coding agent
    to execute.  Previously, the workflow would
    attempt to create a PR directly, which is blocked
    by Microsoft security policies.  Each run failed
    and produced an issue with a helpful "create PR"
    link but could not be customized and broke
    issue triage requiring frequent manual intervention.

* Address PR review feedback for agentic workflows

- Fix dangling 'Conventions below' reference in docs workflow
- Fix package ID ambiguity in deprecation check (check issue body first, directory fallback, skip if ambiguous)
- Add NuGet registration pagination awareness
- Fix 'no longer published' wording to 'no longer supported'
- Strengthen snippet instructions in Implementation Guide (full lifecycle, #if !SNIPPET, expanded validation)
- Renumber deprecation lookup steps after pagination insertion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix flaky HttpClientTransportReferenceCountingTests under coverage (#57789)

Replace Task.Delay timing with deterministic SemaphoreSlim/ManualResetEventSlim
synchronization to ensure requests are in-flight before Update() is called.

Closes #57789

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Wrap synchronization in try/finally to prevent test hangs on failure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use single CancellationTokenSource timeout for semaphore waits

Replaces per-iteration 10s timeouts with a single overall 10s deadline
so failure mode is bounded at 10s total, not N*10s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use single 5s CTS deadline for all synchronization waits

Wire the CancellationToken into both the semaphore waits and the
handler's ManualResetEventSlim.Wait so everything unblocks cleanly
on timeout.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use LongRunning tasks to avoid thread pool starvation in sync variant

In sync mode, each request blocks a thread pool thread. With 10
concurrent requests, the pool needs time to grow, causing timeouts
on CI agents with few cores. Using TaskCreationOptions.LongRunning
creates dedicated threads, avoiding starvation. Timeout set to 10s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>"},"committer":{"avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4","id":19864447,"login":"web-flow","profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow"},"html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/5319471352dd8f43d2f18099a994cdb6a38131fc","sha":"5319471352dd8f43d2f18099a994cdb6a38131fc"},{"author":{"avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/64171366?v=4","id":64171366,"login":"m-nash","profile_url":"https://github.com/m-nash"},"commit":{"author":{"date":"2026-04-03T21:38:43Z","email":"64171366+m-nash@users.noreply.github.com","name":"m-nash"},"committer":{"date":"2026-04-03T21:38:43Z","email":"noreply@github.com","name":"GitHub"},"message":"Add TestDependsOnDependency for packages with cross-service dependents (#57685)

* Add TestDependsOnDependency to CI files for packages with cross-service dependents

When a package is depended on by test projects in other service directories,
changes to that package can break downstream consumers (as seen with the
Storage TypeSpec migration breaking DataFactory tests).

This change:
- Adds TestDependsOnDependency to 38 CI files covering 50 packages that have
  cross-service test dependents. When these packages change, the CI pipeline
  automatically discovers and runs all downstream dependent tests with
  UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true.
- Adds a validation step to CodeChecks.ps1 that detects when a package has
  cross-service dependents but is not covered by TestDependsOnDependency.
  In CI mode it fails with instructions; with -SkipDiffValidation it auto-fixes.
- Adds unit tests for the new validation and auto-fix functions.

Ref: #57679

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ForEach-Object return bug in Get-MissingTestDependsOnDependency

Replace ForEach-Object pipeline with foreach loop so that skipping
the current service directory uses 'continue' instead of 'return',
which would have exited the entire function early.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove unused pkgToSvc mapping in Get-MissingTestDependsOnDependency

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cross-platform path handling for ProjectReference on Linux

Normalize backslashes to forward slashes before calling
GetFileNameWithoutExtension, since Linux does not treat
backslash as a path separator.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace Join-String with -join for PS 5.1 compatibility

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use UTF-8 no-BOM encoding when writing CI YAML files

Avoids PS 5.1 defaulting to UTF-16 with Set-Content, which would
introduce encoding changes and potentially break YAML tooling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add CODEOWNERS entry for CI pipeline config files

Require review from @Azure/azure-sdk-write-net-core for any changes
to ci*.yml files under sdk/, ensuring CI pipeline configuration
changes are reviewed by the core team.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reduce PRJobBatchSize from 10 to 5 for faster CI

Smaller batches run CodeChecks on fewer service directories per job,
reducing the risk of timeouts when many services are changed. This
creates more parallel jobs but each finishes faster.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert "Reduce PRJobBatchSize from 10 to 5 for faster CI"

This reverts commit 183a624b8d1bb93edb2536a3a6dc2c7fad0134ba.

* Reduce PRJobBatchSize from 10 to 5

The Analyze step runs CodeChecks (codegen + snippets + API export)
sequentially for each service in a batch. Heavy services like
websites and compute take 8-15 min each, so batches of 6+ services
can exceed the 60-min job timeout. Reducing to 5 per batch keeps
each job under the timeout while parallelizing across more agents.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Skip codegen/snippets/API export when only ci*.yml files changed

When CodeChecks runs for a service directory where only CI pipeline
config files (ci*.yml) changed, skip the expensive GenerateCode,
Update-Snippets, and Export-API steps since they cannot be affected
by CI config changes. This significantly reduces Analyze job time
for PRs that modify many ci.yml files across service directories.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add try/catch fallback and error handling tests for Test-OnlyCiConfigChanged

Wraps the function in try/catch so any unexpected error (corrupt repo,
permissions, etc.) falls back to returning false (run full codegen).
Adds tests for non-git directory, nonexistent path, and empty input.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Only skip codegen for ci*.yml-only changes in PR builds

Release and CI push pipelines should always run the full codegen,
snippets, and API export checks. Guard the optimization behind
BUILD_REASON == PullRequest so it only applies to PR validation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reduce NumOfTestProjectsPerJob from 20 to 10

The dependency test batches run test projects sequentially. Heavy
test suites like Storage, KeyVault, and Search can cause batches
of 20 projects to exceed the 60-min job timeout. Reducing to 10
creates more parallel jobs that each finish faster.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reduce test PRJobBatchSize from 20 to 10

The test matrix batches packages alphabetically with no weighting.
With 20 per batch, some batches get heavy test suites that exceed
the 60-min timeout on macOS agents. Reducing to 10 creates more
parallel test jobs with fewer packages each.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Ignore ContainerApps Job tests and revert NumOfTestProjectsPerJob

Ignore ContainerAppJobTests.CreateOrUpdate and
ContainerAppJobApiTests.CreateOrUpdate — these fail with
TestRecordingMismatchException due to api-version mismatch
(2023-05-01 recorded vs 2025-07-01 source-built) when running
with UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true. Tracked in #57787.

Revert NumOfTestProjectsPerJob back to 20 as the change had no
effect — this parameter controls the TestDependsOnDependency
split, not the main PR test batching.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

The focus of these changes is to tune behavior of
the issue triage and samples/docs workflows, to
remove friction and enhance functionality.

    identified, validate on NuGet that the package
    is not deprecated. If it is, inform the issue
    author and resolve the issue.

  - Revise the workflow to open an issue with a
    detailed set of instructions for a coding agent
    to execute.  Previously, the workflow would
    attempt to create a PR directly, which is blocked
    by Microsoft security policies.  Each run failed
    and produced an issue with a helpful "create PR"
    link but could not be customized and broke
    issue triage requiring frequent manual intervention.

* Fix flaky HttpClientTransportReferenceCountingTests under coverage (#57789)

Replace Task.Delay timing with deterministic SemaphoreSlim/ManualResetEventSlim
synchronization to ensure requests are in-flight before Update() is called.

Closes #57789

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Wrap synchronization in try/finally to prevent test hangs on failure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use single CancellationTokenSource timeout for semaphore waits

Replaces per-iteration 10s timeouts with a single overall 10s deadline
so failure mode is bounded at 10s total, not N*10s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use single 5s CTS deadline for all synchronization waits

Wire the CancellationToken into both the semaphore waits and the
handler's ManualResetEventSlim.Wait so everything unblocks cleanly
on timeout.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use LongRunning tasks to avoid thread pool starvation in sync variant

In sync mode, each request blocks a thread pool thread. With 10
concurrent requests, the pool needs time to grow, causing timeouts
on CI agents with few cores. Using TaskCreationOptions.LongRunning
creates dedicated threads, avoiding starvation. Timeout set to 10s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add TestDependsOnDependency to CI files for packages with cross-service dependents

When a package is depended on by test projects in other service directories,
changes to that package can break downstream consumers (as seen with the
Storage TypeSpec migration breaking DataFactory tests).

This change:
- Adds TestDependsOnDependency to 38 CI files covering 50 packages that have
  cross-service test dependents. When these packages change, the CI pipeline
  automatically discovers and runs all downstream dependent tests with
  UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true.
- Adds a validation step to CodeChecks.ps1 that detects when a package has
  cross-service dependents but is not covered by TestDependsOnDependency.
  In CI mode it fails with instructions; with -SkipDiffValidation it auto-fixes.
- Adds unit tests for the new validation and auto-fix functions.

Ref: #57679

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ForEach-Object return bug in Get-MissingTestDependsOnDependency

Replace ForEach-Object pipeline with foreach loop so that skipping
the current service directory uses 'continue' instead of 'return',
which would have exited the entire function early.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove unused pkgToSvc mapping in Get-MissingTestDependsOnDependency

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cross-platform path handling for ProjectReference on Linux

Normalize backslashes to forward slashes before calling
GetFileNameWithoutExtension, since Linux does not treat
backslash as a path separator.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace Join-String with -join for PS 5.1 compatibility

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use UTF-8 no-BOM encoding when writing CI YAML files

Avoids PS 5.1 defaulting to UTF-16 with Set-Content, which would
introduce encoding changes and potentially break YAML tooling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add CODEOWNERS entry for CI pipeline config files

Require review from @Azure/azure-sdk-write-net-core for any changes
to ci*.yml files under sdk/, ensuring CI pipeline configuration
changes are reviewed by the core team.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reduce PRJobBatchSize from 10 to 5 for faster CI

Smaller batches run CodeChecks on fewer service directories per job,
reducing the risk of timeouts when many services are changed. This
creates more parallel jobs but each finishes faster.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert "Reduce PRJobBatchSize from 10 to 5 for faster CI"

This reverts commit 183a624b8d1bb93edb2536a3a6dc2c7fad0134ba.

* Reduce PRJobBatchSize from 10 to 5

The Analyze step runs CodeChecks (codegen + snippets + API export)
sequentially for each service in a batch. Heavy services like
websites and compute take 8-15 min each, so batches of 6+ services
can exceed the 60-min job timeout. Reducing to 5 per batch keeps
each job under the timeout while parallelizing across more agents.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Skip codegen/snippets/API export when only ci*.yml files changed

When CodeChecks runs for a service directory where only CI pipeline
config files (ci*.yml) changed, skip the expensive GenerateCode,
Update-Snippets, and Export-API steps since they cannot be affected
by CI config changes. This significantly reduces Analyze job time
for PRs that modify many ci.yml files across service directories.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add try/catch fallback and error handling tests for Test-OnlyCiConfigChanged

Wraps the function in try/catch so any unexpected error (corrupt repo,
permissions, etc.) falls back to returning false (run full codegen).
Adds tests for non-git directory, nonexistent path, and empty input.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Only skip codegen for ci*.yml-only changes in PR builds

Release and CI push pipelines should always run the full codegen,
snippets, and API export checks. Guard the optimization behind
BUILD_REASON == PullRequest so it only applies to PR validation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reduce NumOfTestProjectsPerJob from 20 to 10

The dependency test batches run test projects sequentially. Heavy
test suites like Storage, KeyVault, and Search can cause batches
of 20 projects to exceed the 60-min job timeout. Reducing to 10
creates more parallel jobs that each finish faster.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reduce test PRJobBatchSize from 20 to 10

The test matrix batches packages alphabetically with no weighting.
With 20 per batch, some batches get heavy test suites that exceed
the 60-min timeout on macOS agents. Reducing to 10 creates more
parallel test jobs with fewer packages each.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Ignore ContainerApps Job tests and revert NumOfTestProjectsPerJob

Ignore ContainerAppJobTests.CreateOrUpdate and
ContainerAppJobApiTests.CreateOrUpdate — these fail with
TestRecordingMismatchException due to api-version mismatch
(2023-05-01 recorded vs 2025-07-01 source-built) when running
with UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true. Tracked in #57787.

Revert NumOfTestProjectsPerJob back to 20 as the change had no
effect — this parameter controls the TestDependsOnDependency
split, not the main PR test batching.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix flaky HttpClientTransportReferenceCountingTests under coverage (#57789)

Replace Task.Delay timing with deterministic SemaphoreSlim/ManualResetEventSlim
synchronization to ensure requests are in-flight before Update() is called.

Closes #57789

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Wrap synchronization in try/finally to prevent test hangs on failure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use single CancellationTokenSource timeout for semaphore waits

Replaces per-iteration 10s timeouts with a single overall 10s deadline
so failure mode is bounded at 10s total, not N*10s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use single 5s CTS deadline for all synchronization waits

Wire the CancellationToken into both the semaphore waits and the
handler's ManualResetEventSlim.Wait so everything unblocks cleanly
on timeout.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use LongRunning tasks to avoid thread pool starvation in sync variant

In sync mode, each request blocks a thread pool thread. With 10
concurrent requests, the pool needs time to grow, causing timeouts
on CI agents with few cores. Using TaskCreationOptions.LongRunning
creates dedicated threads, avoiding starvation. Timeout set to 10s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add TestDependsOnDependency to CI files for packages with cross-service dependents

When a package is depended on by test projects in other service directories,
changes to that package can break downstream consumers (as seen with the
Storage TypeSpec migration breaking DataFactory tests).

This change:
- Adds TestDependsOnDependency to 38 CI files covering 50 packages that have
  cross-service test dependents. When these packages change, the CI pipeline
  automatically discovers and runs all downstream dependent tests with
  UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true.
- Adds a validation step to CodeChecks.ps1 that detects when a package has
  cross-service dependents but is not covered by TestDependsOnDependency.
  In CI mode it fails with instructions; with -SkipDiffValidation it auto-fixes.
- Adds unit tests for the new validation and auto-fix functions.

Ref: #57679

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ForEach-Object return bug in Get-MissingTestDependsOnDependency

Replace ForEach-Object pipeline with foreach loop so that skipping
the current service directory uses 'continue' instead of 'return',
which would have exited the entire function early.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove unused pkgToSvc mapping in Get-MissingTestDependsOnDependency

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cross-platform path handling for ProjectReference on Linux

Normalize backslashes to forward slashes before calling
GetFileNameWithoutExtension, since Linux does not treat
backslash as a path separator.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace Join-String with -join for PS 5.1 compatibility

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use UTF-8 no-BOM encoding when writing CI YAML files

Avoids PS 5.1 defaulting to UTF-16 with Set-Content, which would
introduce encoding changes and potentially break YAML tooling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add CODEOWNERS entry for CI pipeline config files

Require review from @Azure/azure-sdk-write-net-core for any changes
to ci*.yml files under sdk/, ensuring CI pipeline configuration
changes are reviewed by the core team.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reduce PRJobBatchSize from 10 to 5 for faster CI

Smaller batches run CodeChecks on fewer service directories per job,
reducing the risk of timeouts when many services are changed. This
creates more parallel jobs but each finishes faster.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert "Reduce PRJobBatchSize from 10 to 5 for faster CI"

This reverts commit 183a624b8d1bb93edb2536a3a6dc2c7fad0134ba.

* Reduce PRJobBatchSize from 10 to 5

The Analyze step runs CodeChecks (codegen + snippets + API export)
sequentially for each service in a batch. Heavy services like
websites and compute take 8-15 min each, so batches of 6+ services
can exceed the 60-min job timeout. Reducing to 5 per batch keeps
each job under the timeout while parallelizing across more agents.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Skip codegen/snippets/API export when only ci*.yml files changed

When CodeChecks runs for a service directory where only CI pipeline
config files (ci*.yml) changed, skip the expensive GenerateCode,
Update-Snippets, and Export-API steps since they cannot be affected
by CI config changes. This significantly reduces Analyze job time
for PRs that modify many ci.yml files across service directories.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add try/catch fallback and error handling tests for Test-OnlyCiConfigChanged

Wraps the function in try/catch so any unexpected error (corrupt repo,
permissions, etc.) falls back to returning false (run full codegen).
Adds tests for non-git directory, nonexistent path, and empty input.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Only skip codegen for ci*.yml-only changes in PR builds

Release and CI push pipelines should always run the full codegen,
snippets, and API export checks. Guard the optimization behind
BUILD_REASON == PullRequest so it only applies to PR validation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reduce NumOfTestProjectsPerJob from 20 to 10

The dependency test batches run test projects sequentially. Heavy
test suites like Storage, KeyVault, and Search can cause batches
of 20 projects to exceed the 60-min job timeout. Reducing to 10
creates more parallel jobs that each finish faster.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reduce test PRJobBatchSize from 20 to 10

The test matrix batches packages alphabetically with no weighting.
With 20 per batch, some batches get heavy test suites that exceed
the 60-min timeout on macOS agents. Reducing to 10 creates more
parallel test jobs with fewer packages each.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Ignore ContainerApps Job tests and revert NumOfTestProjectsPerJob

Ignore ContainerAppJobTests.CreateOrUpdate and
ContainerAppJobApiTests.CreateOrUpdate — these fail with
TestRecordingMismatchException due to api-version mismatch
(2023-05-01 recorded vs 2025-07-01 source-built) when running
with UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true. Tracked in #57787.

Revert NumOfTestProjectsPerJob back to 20 as the change had no
effect — this parameter controls the TestDependsOnDependency
split, not the main PR test batching.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: 967674ae203662c6afdd96f808b969843a67913e)","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"# Azure Authoring client library for .NET

Azure Conversations Authoring is part of the Azure Cognitive Service for Language, a cloud-based service that provides tools for creating, managing, and deploying conversational AI solutions. This client library offers the following features:

* Creating and managing conversation projects
* Importing conversation projects
* Exporting conversation projects
* Getting project details
* Deleting projects
* Training models for conversational AI
* Canceling active training jobs
* Evaluating model summaries
* Evaluating model results
* Managing project snapshots
* Loading snapshots
* Deploying conversational AI models
* Swapping deployments for active models
* Deleting trained models
* Deleting deployments
* Assigning project resources
* Getting deployment resource assignment status
* Unassigning project resources
* Getting deployment resource unassignment status

[Source code][source_root] | [Package (NuGet)][package]| [API reference documentation][text_refdocs] | [Product documentation][text_docs] | [Samples][source_samples]

## Getting started

This section should include everything a developer needs to do to install and create their first client connection *very quickly*.

### Install the package

Install the client library for .NET with [NuGet](https://www.nuget.org/ ):

```dotnetcli
dotnet add package Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring --prerelease
```

|SDK version  |Supported API version of service
|-------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------
|1.0.0-beta.1 | 2023-04-01, 2023-04-15-preview, 2024-11-15-preview (default)
|1.0.0-beta.2 | 2023-04-01, 2023-04-15-preview, 2024-11-15-preview, 2025-05-15-preview (default)
|1.0.0-beta.3 | 2023-04-01, 2025-11-01, 2025-05-15-preview, 2025-11-15-preview (default)

### Prerequisites

* An [Azure subscription][azure_sub].
* An existing Cognitive Services or Language service resource.

### Authenticate the client

In order to interact with the Conversations Authoring service, you'll need to create an instance of the [`ConversationAnalysisAuthoring`][ConversationAnalysisAuthoring_class] class. You will need an **endpoint**, and an **API key** to instantiate a client object. For more information regarding authenticating with Cognitive Services, see [Authenticate requests to Azure Cognitive Services][cognitive_auth].

#### Get an API key

You can get the `endpoint` and `API key` from the Cognitive Services resource or Language service resource information in the [Azure Portal][azure_portal].

Alternatively, use the [Azure CLI][azure_cli] snippet below to get the API key from the Language service resource.

```PowerShell
az cognitiveservices account keys list --resource-group <your-resource-group-name> --name <your-resource-name>
```

#### Create a AnalyzeConversationAuthoring Client

To use the AnalyzeConversationAuthoring client, include the following namespace in your project:

```C#
using Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring;
```

With your endpoint and API key, you can instantiate a `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` client using specific service options:

```C# Snippet:CreateAuthoringClientForSpecificApiVersion
Uri endpoint = new Uri("{endpoint}");
AzureKeyCredential credential = new AzureKeyCredential("{api-key}");
ConversationAnalysisAuthoringClientOptions options = new ConversationAnalysisAuthoringClientOptions(ConversationAnalysisAuthoringClientOptions.ServiceVersion.V2025_11_15_Preview);
ConversationAnalysisAuthoring client = new ConversationAnalysisAuthoring(endpoint, credential, options);
```

#### Create a client using Azure Active Directory authentication

You can also create a `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` client using Azure Active Directory (AAD) authentication. Your user or service principal must be assigned the "Cognitive Services Language Reader" role.
Using the [DefaultAzureCredential] you can authenticate a service using Managed Identity or a service principal, authenticate as a developer working on an application, and more, all without changing code.

Before you can use the `DefaultAzureCredential`, or any credential type from [Azure.Identity][azure_identity], you'll first need to [install the Azure.Identity package][azure_identity_install].

To use `DefaultAzureCredential` with a client ID and secret, you'll need to set the `AZURE_TENANT_ID`, `AZURE_CLIENT_ID`, and `AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET` environment variables; alternatively, you can pass those values
to the `ClientSecretCredential` also in Azure.Identity.

Make sure you use the right namespace for `DefaultAzureCredential` at the top of your source file:

```C# Snippet:Conversations_Identity_Namespace
using Azure.Identity;
using Azure.Core;
```

Then you can create an instance of `DefaultAzureCredential` and pass it to a new instance of your client:

```C# Snippet:AnalyzeConversationAuthoring_CreateWithDefaultAzureCredential
Uri endpoint = new Uri("{endpoint}");
DefaultAzureCredential credential = new DefaultAzureCredential();
ConversationAnalysisAuthoring client = new ConversationAnalysisAuthoring(endpoint, credential);
```

Note that regional endpoints do not support AAD authentication. Instead, create a [custom domain][custom_domain] name for your resource to use AAD authentication.

### Service API versions

The client library targets the latest service API version by default. A client instance accepts an optional service API version parameter from its options to specify which API version service to communicate.

#### Select a service API version

You have the flexibility to explicitly select a supported service API version when instantiating a client by configuring its associated options. This ensures that the client can communicate with services using the specified API version.

For example,

```C# Snippet:CreateAuthoringClientForSpecificApiVersion
Uri endpoint = new Uri("{endpoint}");
AzureKeyCredential credential = new AzureKeyCredential("{api-key}");
ConversationAnalysisAuthoringClientOptions options = new ConversationAnalysisAuthoringClientOptions(ConversationAnalysisAuthoringClientOptions.ServiceVersion.V2025_11_15_Preview);
ConversationAnalysisAuthoring client = new ConversationAnalysisAuthoring(endpoint, credential, options);
```

When selecting an API version, it's important to verify that there are no breaking changes compared to the latest API version. If there are significant differences, API calls may fail due to incompatibility.

Always ensure that the chosen API version is fully supported and operational for your specific use case and that it aligns with the service's versioning policy.

If you do not select an api version we will default to the latest version available, which has the possibility of being a preview version.

## Key concepts

### ConversationAnalysisAuthoring Client

The [ConversationAnalysisAuthoring][ConversationAnalysisAuthoring_class] is the primary client for developers using the Azure AI Conversation Authoring client library. It provides both synchronous and asynchronous operations to access conversation authoring functionality, such as creating and managing conversation projects, deployments, trained models, and exported models.

### Thread safety

We guarantee that all client instance methods are thread-safe and independent of each other ([guideline](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html#dotnet-service-methods-thread-safety)). This ensures that the recommendation of reusing client instances is always safe, even across threads.

## Troubleshooting

### General

When you interact with the Cognitive Language Services Conversations Authoring client library using the .NET SDK, errors returned by the service correspond to the same HTTP status codes returned for REST API requests.

For example, if you attempt to create a project with an invalid configuration, a 400 error is returned indicating "Bad Request".

```C# Snippet:AuthoringClient_BadRequest
try
{
    string invalidProjectName = "InvalidProject";
    ConversationAuthoringCreateProjectDetails projectData = new ConversationAuthoringCreateProjectDetails(
      projectKind: "Conversation",
      projectName: invalidProjectName,
      language: "invalid-lang"
    )
    {
        Description = "This is a test for invalid configuration."
    };
    using RequestContent content = RequestContent.Create(projectData);
    Response response = client.CreateProject(invalidProjectName, content);
}
catch (RequestFailedException ex)
{
    Console.WriteLine(ex.ToString());
}
```

You will notice that additional information is logged, like the client request ID of the operation.

```text
Azure.RequestFailedException: The input parameter is invalid.
Status: 400 (Bad Request)
ErrorCode: InvalidArgument

Content:
Azure.RequestFailedException: Invalid Request.
Status: 400 (Bad Request)
ErrorCode: InvalidRequest

Content:
{"error":{"code":"InvalidRequest","message":"Invalid Request.","innererror":{"code":"LanguageCodeInvalid","message":"The language code is invalid. Possible values are: en, es, fr, ..."}}}

Headers:
Transfer-Encoding: chunked
x-envoy-upstream-service-time: REDACTED
apim-request-id: REDACTED
Strict-Transport-Security: REDACTED
X-Content-Type-Options: REDACTED
x-ms-region: REDACTED
Date: Wed, 24 Jul 2024 13:39:00 GMT
Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8
```

### Setting up console logging

The simplest way to see the logs is to enable the console logging.
To create an Azure SDK log listener that outputs messages to console use AzureEventSourceListener.CreateConsoleLogger method.

```C#
// Setup a listener to monitor logged events.
using AzureEventSourceListener listener = AzureEventSourceListener.CreateConsoleLogger();
```

To learn more about other logging mechanisms see [here][logging].

See the [CONTRIBUTING.md][contributing] for details on building, testing, and contributing to this library.

### Install the package

dotnet add package Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring --prerelease
```

|SDK version  |Supported API version of service

|1.0.0-beta.2 | 2023-04-01, 2023-04-15-preview, 2024-11-15-preview, 2025-05-15-preview (default)

### Prerequisites

* An [Azure subscription][azure_sub].
* An existing Cognitive Services or Language service resource.

### Authenticate the client

In order to interact with the Conversations Authoring service, you'll need to create an instance of the [`ConversationAnalysisAuthoring`][ConversationAnalysisAuthoring_class] class. You will need an **endpoint**, and an **API key** to instantiate a client object. For more information regarding authenticating with Cognitive Services, see [Authenticate requests to Azure Cognitive Services][cognitive_auth].

#### Get an API key

You can get the `endpoint` and `API key` from the Cognitive Services resource or Language service resource information in the [Azure Portal][azure_portal].

Alternatively, use the [Azure CLI][azure_cli] snippet below to get the API key from the Language service resource.

```PowerShell
az cognitiveservices account keys list --resource-group <your-resource-group-name> --name <your-resource-name>
```

#### Create a AnalyzeConversationAuthoring Client

To use the AnalyzeConversationAuthoring client, include the following namespace in your project:

```C#
using Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring;
```

With your endpoint and API key, you can instantiate a `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` client using specific service options:

```C# Snippet:CreateAuthoringClientForSpecificApiVersion
Uri endpoint = new Uri("{endpoint}");
AzureKeyCredential credential = new AzureKeyCredential("{api-key}");
ConversationAnalysisAuthoringClientOptions options = new ConversationAnalysisAuthoringClientOptions(ConversationAnalysisAuthoringClientOptions.ServiceVersion.V2025_11_15_Preview);
ConversationAnalysisAuthoring client = new ConversationAnalysisAuthoring(endpoint, credential, options);
```

#### Create a client using Azure Active Directory authentication

You can also create a `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` client using Azure Active Directory (AAD) authentication. Your user or service principal must be assigned the "Cognitive Services Language Reader" role.
Using the [DefaultAzureCredential] you can authenticate a service using Managed Identity or a service principal, authenticate as a developer working on an application, and more, all without changing code.

Before you can use the `DefaultAzureCredential`, or any credential type from [Azure.Identity][azure_identity], you'll first need to [install the Azure.Identity package][azure_identity_install].

To use `DefaultAzureCredential` with a client ID and secret, you'll need to set the `AZURE_TENANT_ID`, `AZURE_CLIENT_ID`, and `AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET` environment variables; alternatively, you can pass those values
to the `ClientSecretCredential` also in Azure.Identity.

Make sure you use the right namespace for `DefaultAzureCredential` at the top of your source file:

```C# Snippet:Conversations_Identity_Namespace

using Azure.Core;
```

Then you can create an instance of `DefaultAzureCredential` and pass it to a new instance of your client:

## Key concepts

### ConversationAnalysisAuthoring Client

The [ConversationAnalysisAuthoring][ConversationAnalysisAuthoring_class] is the primary client for developers using the Azure AI Conversation Authoring client library. It provides both synchronous and asynchronous operations to access conversation authoring functionality, such as creating and managing conversation projects, deployments, trained models, and exported models.

### Thread safety

We guarantee that all client instance methods are thread-safe and independent of each other ([guideline](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html#dotnet-service-methods-thread-safety)). This ensures that the recommendation of reusing client instances is always safe, even across threads.

### General

When you interact with the Cognitive Language Services Conversations Authoring client library using the .NET SDK, errors returned by the service correspond to the same HTTP status codes returned for REST API requests.

For example, if you attempt to create a project with an invalid configuration, a 400 error is returned indicating "Bad Request".

```C# Snippet:AuthoringClient_BadRequest
try
{
    string invalidProjectName = "InvalidProject";
    ConversationAuthoringCreateProjectDetails projectData = new ConversationAuthoringCreateProjectDetails(
      projectKind: "Conversation",
      projectName: invalidProjectName,
      language: "invalid-lang"
    )
    {
        Description = "This is a test for invalid configuration."
    };
    using RequestContent content = RequestContent.Create(projectData);
    Response response = client.CreateProject(invalidProjectName, content);
}
catch (RequestFailedException ex)

dotnet add package Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring --prerelease
```

### Prerequisites

* An [Azure subscription][azure_sub].
* An existing Cognitive Services or Language service resource.

### Authenticate the client

In order to interact with the Conversations Authoring service, you'll need to create an instance of the [`ConversationAnalysisAuthoring`][ConversationAnalysisAuthoring_class] class. You will need an **endpoint**, and an **API key** to instantiate a client object. For more information regarding authenticating with Cognitive Services, see [Authenticate requests to Azure Cognitive Services][cognitive_auth].

#### Get an API key

You can get the `endpoint` and `API key` from the Cognitive Services resource or Language service resource information in the [Azure Portal][azure_portal].

Alternatively, use the [Azure CLI][azure_cli] snippet below to get the API key from the Language service resource.

```PowerShell
az cognitiveservices account keys list --resource-group <your-resource-group-name> --name <your-resource-name>
```

#### Create a AnalyzeConversationAuthoring Client

To use the AnalyzeConversationAuthoring client, include the following namespace in your project:

```C#
using Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring;
```

With your endpoint and API key, you can instantiate a `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` client using specific service options:

```C# Snippet:CreateAuthoringClientForSpecificApiVersion
Uri endpoint = new Uri("{endpoint}");
AzureKeyCredential credential = new AzureKeyCredential("{api-key}");
ConversationAnalysisAuthoringClientOptions options = new ConversationAnalysisAuthoringClientOptions(ConversationAnalysisAuthoringClientOptions.ServiceVersion.V2025_11_15_Preview);
ConversationAnalysisAuthoring client = new ConversationAnalysisAuthoring(endpoint, credential, options);
```

#### Create a client using Azure Active Directory authentication

You can also create a `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` client using Azure Active Directory (AAD) authentication. Your user or service principal must be assigned the "Cognitive Services Language Reader" role.
Using the [DefaultAzureCredential] you can authenticate a service using Managed Identity or a service principal, authenticate as a developer working on an application, and more, all without changing code.

Before you can use the `DefaultAzureCredential`, or any credential type from [Azure.Identity][azure_identity], you'll first need to [install the Azure.Identity package][azure_identity_install].

To use `DefaultAzureCredential` with a client ID and secret, you'll need to set the `AZURE_TENANT_ID`, `AZURE_CLIENT_ID`, and `AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET` environment variables; alternatively, you can pass those values
to the `ClientSecretCredential` also in Azure.Identity.

Make sure you use the right namespace for `DefaultAzureCredential` at the top of your source file:

```C# Snippet:Conversations_Identity_Namespace
using Azure.Identity;
using Azure.Core;
```

Then you can create an instance of `DefaultAzureCredential` and pass it to a new instance of your client:

```C# Snippet:AnalyzeConversationAuthoring_CreateWithDefaultAzureCredential
Uri endpoint = new Uri("{endpoint}");
DefaultAzureCredential credential = new DefaultAzureCredential();
ConversationAnalysisAuthoring client = new ConversationAnalysisAuthoring(endpoint, credential);
```

Note that regional endpoints do not support AAD authentication. Instead, create a [custom domain][custom_domain] name for your resource to use AAD authentication.

### Service API versions

The client library targets the latest service API version by default. A client instance accepts an optional service API version parameter from its options to specify which API version service to communicate.

#### Select a service API version

You have the flexibility to explicitly select a supported service API version when instantiating a client by configuring its associated options. This ensures that the client can communicate with services using the specified API version.

For example,

```C# Snippet:CreateAuthoringClientForSpecificApiVersion
Uri endpoint = new Uri("{endpoint}");
AzureKeyCredential credential = new AzureKeyCredential("{api-key}");

ConversationAnalysisAuthoring client = new ConversationAnalysisAuthoring(endpoint, credential, options);
```

When selecting an API version, it's important to verify that there are no breaking changes compared to the latest API version. If there are significant differences, API calls may fail due to incompatibility.

Always ensure that the chosen API version is fully supported and operational for your specific use case and that it aligns with the service's versioning policy.

If you do not select an api version we will default to the latest version available, which has the possibility of being a preview version.

## Key concepts

### ConversationAnalysisAuthoring Client

The [ConversationAnalysisAuthoring][ConversationAnalysisAuthoring_class] is the primary client for developers using the Azure AI Conversation Authoring client library. It provides both synchronous and asynchronous operations to access conversation authoring functionality, such as creating and managing conversation projects, deployments, trained models, and exported models.

### Thread safety

We guarantee that all client instance methods are thread-safe and independent of each other ([guideline](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html#dotnet-service-methods-thread-safety)). This ensures that the recommendation of reusing client instances is always safe, even across threads.

## Troubleshooting

### General

When you interact with the Cognitive Language Services Conversations Authoring client library using the .NET SDK, errors returned by the service correspond to the same HTTP status codes returned for REST API requests.

For example, if you attempt to create a project with an invalid configuration, a 400 error is returned indicating "Bad Request".

```C# Snippet:AuthoringClient_BadRequest
try
{
    string invalidProjectName = "InvalidProject";
    ConversationAuthoringCreateProjectDetails projectData = new ConversationAuthoringCreateProjectDetails(
      projectKind: "Conversation",
      projectName: invalidProjectName,
      language: "invalid-lang"
    )
    {
        Description = "This is a test for invalid configuration."
    };
    using RequestContent content = RequestContent.Create(projectData);
    Response response = client.CreateProject(invalidProjectName, content);
}
catch (RequestFailedException ex)
{
    Console.WriteLine(ex.ToString());
}
```

arguments.title: [Conversations.Authoring] Docs: Fix broken links and missing inline examples in README
arguments.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring` **Service directory:** `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/` **Triggered by:** PR #57627 by @radhgupta ## What Changed PR #57627 migrated the `Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring` package to the new TypeSpec emitter, regenerating client code, updating the README, and adding/updating 23 sample `.md` files. The README was substantially updated but several gaps remain. ## Gaps Found - **Template placeholder not removed**: The `## Getting started` section still contains the boilerplate text `"This section should include everything a developer needs to do to install and create their first client connection *very quickly*."` — this must be removed. - **Broken NuGet package link**: `[package]: https://www.nuget.org/packages/` is missing the package name; should be `https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/`. - **Broken API reference link**: `[text_refdocs]: https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/` is incomplete and does not resolve to the actual API docs. - **No inline code snippet in the Examples section**: The `## Examples` section only lists links to external `.md` sample files. The canonical README pattern includes at least one inline `C# Snippet:` example (backed by a test file) so users see a quick-start code block directly in the README without navigating away.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/README.md` — edit **Change 1 — Remove template placeholder** Find and remove this line under `## Getting started`: ```text This section should include everything a developer needs to do to install and create their first client connection *very quickly*. ``` **Change 2 — Fix NuGet package link** (bottom of file, `` section) Replace: ```text [package]: https://www.nuget.org/packages/ ``` With: ```text [package]: https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/ ``` **Change 3 — Fix API reference link** (bottom of file, `` section) Replace: ```text [text_refdocs]: https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ ``` With: ```text [text_refdocs]: https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/azure.ai.language.conversations.authoring?view=azure-dotnet-preview ``` **Change 4 — Add inline snippet placeholder in `## Examples`** After the list of sample links in `## Examples`, add a short "Create a project" quick-start snippet placeholder: ```markdown ### Create a project ```C# Snippet:ConversationsAuthoring_CreateProject ``` ``` (closing triple-backtick on its own line) ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The test file `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/tests/Samples/Sample1_ConversationsAuthoring_CreateProject.cs` likely already contains a sample method. Wrap the relevant portion in a region so the snippet generator can extract it: ```csharp #region Snippet:ConversationsAuthoring_CreateProject // ... the core create-project code (no test setup, assertions, or cleanup) ... #endregion ``` Any test-only code (setup, teardown, assertions) must be wrapped in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif` so it is excluded from the rendered README. Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` as a canonical example of the `#region Snippet:` / `#if !SNIPPET` pattern. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently contains all required sections in the correct order: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Authenticate) 2. ✅ Key concepts 3. ✅ Examples — **needs inline snippet added** (see above) 4. ✅ Troubleshooting 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing No structural changes are needed beyond the four edits above. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cognitivelanguage` — confirm the `## Examples` snippet block in README.md is populated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 cognitivelanguage` — no public API changes expected; verify no diff 5. `dotnet format sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/`
## Service Contacts @ahmedaashour @bidisha-c @mikaelsitruk @quentinRobinson @sarkar-rajarshi ### Suggested next steps Consider assigning this issue to **Copilot coding agent** for implementation.

arguments.title: [Conversations.Authoring] Docs: Fix broken links and missing inline examples in README
arguments.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring` **Service directory:** `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/` **Triggered by:** PR #57627 by @radhgupta ## What Changed PR #57627 migrated the `Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring` package to the new TypeSpec emitter, regenerating client code, updating the README, and adding/updating 23 sample `.md` files. The README was substantially updated but several gaps remain. ## Gaps Found - **Template placeholder not removed**: The `## Getting started` section still contains the boilerplate text `"This section should include everything a developer needs to do to install and create their first client connection *very quickly*."` — this must be removed. - **Broken NuGet package link**: `[package]: https://www.nuget.org/packages/` is missing the package name; should be `https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/`. - **Broken API reference link**: `[text_refdocs]: https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/` is incomplete and does not resolve to the actual API docs. - **No inline code snippet in the Examples section**: The `## Examples` section only lists links to external `.md` sample files. The canonical README pattern includes at least one inline `C# Snippet:` example (backed by a test file) so users see a quick-start code block directly in the README without navigating away.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/README.md` — edit **Change 1 — Remove template placeholder** Find and remove this line under `## Getting started`: ```text This section should include everything a developer needs to do to install and create their first client connection *very quickly*. ``` **Change 2 — Fix NuGet package link** (bottom of file, `` section) Replace: ```text [package]: https://www.nuget.org/packages/ ``` With: ```text [package]: https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/ ``` **Change 3 — Fix API reference link** (bottom of file, `` section) Replace: ```text [text_refdocs]: https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ ``` With: ```text [text_refdocs]: https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/azure.ai.language.conversations.authoring?view=azure-dotnet-preview ``` **Change 4 — Add inline snippet placeholder in `## Examples`** After the list of sample links in `## Examples`, add a short "Create a project" quick-start snippet placeholder: ```markdown ### Create a project ```C# Snippet:ConversationsAuthoring_CreateProject ``` ``` (closing triple-backtick on its own line) ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The test file `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/tests/Samples/Sample1_ConversationsAuthoring_CreateProject.cs` likely already contains a sample method. Wrap the relevant portion in a region so the snippet generator can extract it: ```csharp #region Snippet:ConversationsAuthoring_CreateProject // ... the core create-project code (no test setup, assertions, or cleanup) ... #endregion ``` Any test-only code (setup, teardown, assertions) must be wrapped in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif` so it is excluded from the rendered README. Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` as a canonical example of the `#region Snippet:` / `#if !SNIPPET` pattern. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently contains all required sections in the correct order: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Authenticate) 2. ✅ Key concepts 3. ✅ Examples — **needs inline snippet added** (see above) 4. ✅ Troubleshooting 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing No structural changes are needed beyond the four edits above. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cognitivelanguage` — confirm the `## Examples` snippet block in README.md is populated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 cognitivelanguage` — no public API changes expected; verify no diff 5. `dotnet format sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/`
## Service Contacts @ahmedaashour @bidisha-c @mikaelsitruk @quentinRobinson @sarkar-rajarshi ### Suggested next steps Consider assigning this issue to **Copilot coding agent** for implementation.

data.content:
Filed GitHub issue: **[Conversations.Authoring] Docs: Fix broken links and missing inline examples in README**

The issue covers four gaps found in the README after the TypeSpec emitter migration (PR #57627):
1. Leftover template placeholder text in `## Getting started`
2. Incomplete NuGet package link
3. Incomplete API reference link
4. No inline `C# Snippet:` example in the `## Examples` section
